 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wit","title":"Research Destructive Command Guard (DCG) source code","description":"# Task: Research Destructive Command Guard (DCG) Source Code

## Purpose
Review DCG source code for /tldr page content.

## Source Location
- Path: /data/projects/destructive_command_guard
- Language: Go
- GitHub: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard

## Research Questions

### Functionality
1. What dangerous commands are intercepted and blocked?
2. How does command interception work?
3. What alternative suggestions are provided?
4. Platform support (Linux, macOS, WSL)?

### Implementation
1. Hook mechanism for Claude Code/Gemini CLI?
2. Pattern matching approach?
3. Configuration options?

### Integrations
1. How does it integrate with Claude Code hooks?
2. NTM integration?

## Deliverable
Structured summary emphasizing safety and prevention of catastrophic commands.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:17:51.912497797-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:17:51.912497797-05:00"}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","title":"Add microinteractions and polish to all /tldr components","description":"# Task: Add Microinteractions and Polish to All /tldr Components

## Overview
After core components are built, this task adds the finishing touches that make 
the page feel premium. Every interactive element should have thoughtful feedback.

## Microinteractions Checklist

### TldrToolCard
- [ ] Hover: subtle scale (1.01-1.02), shadow deepens
- [ ] Hover: border glow in tool's accent color
- [ ] Click/tap: brief scale down (active state)
- [ ] Expand: smooth height animation with spring physics
- [ ] Collapse: smooth with slight overshoot
- [ ] Stars badge: pulse animation on mount
- [ ] GitHub link: arrow slides right on hover
- [ ] Synergy links: subtle highlight on hover

### TldrToolGrid
- [ ] Stagger entrance: each card appears 50ms after previous
- [ ] Category headers: fade in before cards
- [ ] Scroll reveal: cards animate when scrolling into view

### TldrSynergyDiagram
- [ ] Node hover: scale up, glow effect
- [ ] Connection hover: line thickens, particles accelerate
- [ ] Click: smooth pan/zoom to focused tool
- [ ] Particle flow: continuous, subtle, mesmerizing
- [ ] Idle state: gentle pulsing/breathing effect

### TldrHero
- [ ] Text entrance: character-by-character or word-by-word
- [ ] Visual element: continuous rotation/motion
- [ ] CTA button: gradient shift on hover
- [ ] Scroll indicator: gentle bounce animation

### Global
- [ ] Page transitions: smooth, no layout shift
- [ ] Loading states: skeleton with shimmer
- [ ] Focus rings: visible, styled to match design
- [ ] Scroll: smooth scroll for all anchor links

## Animation Principles
- **Duration**: 200-400ms for most interactions
- **Easing**: Spring physics for playful, linear for continuous
- **Delay**: Use stagger, not simultaneous
- **Subtlety**: Enhance, don't distract

## Implementation Notes
- Use Framer Motion variants for consistency
- Create shared animation config file
- Test at 30fps (throttle CPU) to ensure smoothness
- Use transform and opacity only (GPU accelerated)

## Reduced Motion Support
All animations must have reduced motion fallbacks:
```tsx
const variants = {
  initial: prefersReducedMotion ? {} : { opacity: 0, y: 20 },
  animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
};
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every interactive element has hover/focus state
- [ ] Animations feel natural and polished
- [ ] No jank or dropped frames
- [ ] Reduced motion respected
- [ ] Consistent animation language across page","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:15.777255988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:15.777255988-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:24.178914299-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-gy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:24.200817906-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uw1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:24.224914944-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1lj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:16:24.247390168-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

### Global

- [ ] Loading states: skeleton with shimmer

## Animation Principles
- **Duration**: 200-400ms for most interactions
- **Easing**: Spring physics for playful, linear for continuous

- **Subtlety**: Enhance, don't distract

## Implementation Notes
- Use Framer Motion variants for consistency
- Create shared animation config file
- Test at 30fps (throttle CPU) to ensure smoothness
- Use transform and opacity only (GPU accelerated)

## Reduced Motion Support
All animations must have reduced motion fallbacks:

const variants = {

  animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
};

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No jank or dropped frames
- [ ] Reduced motion respected

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wxr","title":"Task: Update existing E2E tests for TL;DR page changes","description":"# Update Existing E2E Tests

## Critical: Tests Will Break
The existing tests in `tests/e2e/tldr.spec.ts` will FAIL after our changes:

### Line 70: Hardcoded "7 Core Tools" check
```typescript
await expect(page.locator("text=7 Core Tools")).toBeVisible();
```
This will fail if we change the count or make it dynamic.

### Line 19: Hardcoded "3,600+" check
```typescript
await expect(page.getByText("3,600+")).toBeVisible();
```
This will fail if we implement live star counts.

### Line 114: Star badge selector
```typescript
const starBadge = page.locator('.bg-amber-500\\/10').first();
```
May need updating if we change star badge styling.

## Required Updates

### 1. Make Core Tools Count Dynamic
```typescript
// BEFORE (brittle)
await expect(page.locator("text=7 Core Tools")).toBeVisible();

// AFTER (robust)
test("should display synergy diagram", async ({ page }) => {
  console.log('[E2E] Testing synergy diagram visibility');
  const diagram = page.locator('svg[aria-label*="synergy diagram"]');
  await expect(diagram).toBeVisible();
  console.log('[E2E] Diagram visible, checking center label');
  
  await expect(page.locator("text=Flywheel")).toBeVisible();
  
  // Dynamic check - just verify pattern "N Core Tools" exists
  const coreToolsLabel = page.locator('text=/\\d+ Core Tools/');
  await expect(coreToolsLabel).toBeVisible();
  console.log('[E2E] Core tools label found');
});
```

### 2. Make Star Count Flexible
```typescript
// BEFORE
await expect(page.getByText("3,600+")).toBeVisible();

// AFTER
test("should render the hero section", async ({ page }) => {
  console.log('[E2E] Testing hero section');
  
  await expect(
    page.getByRole("heading", { name: /the agentic coding flywheel/i })
  ).toBeVisible();
  
  // Check stats exist and have numeric values
  const statsSection = page.locator('[class*="flex"][class*="gap"]').filter({
    has: page.locator('text=/\\d+/')
  });
  
  // Verify Tools stat
  console.log('[E2E] Checking Tools stat');
  await expect(page.getByText("13")).toBeVisible();
  
  // Verify Stars stat - flexible pattern for K format or raw number
  console.log('[E2E] Checking Stars stat (flexible pattern)');
  const starsValue = page.locator('text=/[\\d,]+\\+?|[\\d.]+K\\+?/');
  await expect(starsValue.first()).toBeVisible();
  
  console.log('[E2E] Hero section tests passed');
});
```

### 3. Add Logging Throughout
Every existing test should have console.log statements:
```typescript
test("should expand and collapse tool cards", async ({ page }) => {
  console.log('[E2E] Starting expand/collapse test');
  
  const showMoreButton = page.getByRole("button", { name: /show more/i }).first();
  console.log('[E2E] Found Show More button, clicking');
  await showMoreButton.click();
  
  console.log('[E2E] Waiting for expansion animation');
  await expect(page.getByText(/why it's useful/i).first()).toBeVisible();
  console.log('[E2E] Expanded content visible');
  
  const showLessButton = page.getByRole("button", { name: /show less/i }).first();
  console.log('[E2E] Found Show Less button, clicking to collapse');
  await showLessButton.click();
  
  console.log('[E2E] Waiting for collapse');
  await expect(page.getByText(/why it's useful/i).first()).not.toBeVisible();
  console.log('[E2E] Expand/collapse test passed');
});
```

## Full List of Tests to Update

1. `should render the hero section` - Flexible star count
2. `should display synergy diagram` - Dynamic core tools count
3. `should display tool stars count` - May need selector update
4. All tests - Add detailed logging

## New Assertions to Add to Existing Tests
```typescript
// In mobile viewport test, verify touch targets
test("should work on mobile viewport", async ({ page }) => {
  console.log('[E2E] Testing mobile viewport');
  await page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 667 });
  await page.reload();
  
  // Existing checks...
  
  // NEW: Verify touch targets are adequate
  console.log('[E2E] Checking touch target sizes');
  const buttons = page.getByRole('button');
  const firstButton = buttons.first();
  const box = await firstButton.boundingBox();
  if (box) {
    console.log(`[E2E] Button size: ${box.width}x${box.height}`);
    expect(box.width).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(44);
    expect(box.height).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(44);
  }
});
```

## Running Updated Tests
```bash
# Run all E2E tests with logging
bunx playwright test tests/e2e/tldr.spec.ts --reporter=list

# Run specific test
bunx playwright test tests/e2e/tldr.spec.ts -g "synergy diagram"

# Run with debug mode
PWDEBUG=1 bunx playwright test tests/e2e/tldr.spec.ts
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All existing tests pass after changes
- [ ] Dynamic assertions replace hardcoded values
- [ ] Every test has console.log statements
- [ ] Tests log start, key actions, and completion
- [ ] No flaky tests (run 3x to verify)
- [ ] Tests run in <30 seconds total","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:30:23.205405083-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:25:06.659920423-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:25:06.659920423-05:00","close_reason":"Updated tests/e2e/tldr.spec.ts with flexible patterns for star counts and core tools count, added detailed console.log statements throughout all tests"}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wyj","title":"Add jeffreysprompts.com to content.ts (19 stars)","description":"## What
Add jeffreysprompts.com project to content.ts

## Project Details
- **Name**: jeffreysprompts.com
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/jeffreysprompts.com
- **Stars**: 19
- **Description**: "A curated collection of battle-tested prompts for agentic coding - Browse, copy, and install as Claude Code skills"

## Background
- Website/app for sharing effective AI coding prompts
- Prompts are "battle-tested" - refined through actual use
- Can be installed as Claude Code skills
- Part of Jeffrey's knowledge-sharing in the AI coding space
- Research at /data/projects/jeffreysprompts.com/

## Implementation
```typescript
{
  name: "Jeffrey's Prompts",
  kind: "oss",  // or "product" if it's a live site
  badge: "19 stars",
  href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/jeffreysprompts.com",
  short: "Curated, battle-tested prompts for AI coding agents.",
  description: "A collection of refined prompts for agentic coding workflows. Browse, copy, and install directly as Claude Code skills. Distilled from extensive real-world AI coding experience.",
  tags: ["Prompts", "Claude", "Skills", "Web", "Education"],
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project added with description emphasizing the practical, tested nature","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T10:25:52.844138471-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T11:20:27.219334825-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T11:20:27.219334825-05:00","close_reason":"Added projects to content.ts"}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-x13","title":"Integrate GitHub heartbeat into site","description":"## Overview
Add the GitHub heartbeat widget to one or more locations on the site.

## Proposed Locations
1. **Hero Section**: Small indicator showing 'actively coding'
2. **Stats Grid**: Replace or augment existing GitHub stats
3. **Footer**: Subtle activity indicator
4. **About Page**: More detailed activity view

## Integration Approach
- Start with stats grid integration (least disruptive)
- Use existing stats-grid.tsx component pattern
- Fetch data server-side with React Server Components
- Pass to client heartbeat component

## Considerations
- Don't make it too prominent (it's a nice-to-have detail)
- Should complement, not compete with, other content
- Consider making it a subtle hover-reveal

## Dependencies
- GitHub API integration (for data)
- Heartbeat visual component (for display)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Widget appears in at least one location
- [ ] Data updates appropriately
- [ ] Doesn't negatively impact page load
- [ ] Visually cohesive with existing design","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:09:47.572748567-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:18.743423069-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-x13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-27n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:12:12.623522501-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-x13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:12:12.655233582-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
